sections.paragraph:
Χαλδαῖος, ου, ὁ (Hdt. et al., both as a name for an inhabitant of Χαλδαία and as a designation for astrologers and interpreters of dreams; TestSol 15:8; TestNapht, ParJer, SibOr, Philo, Joseph., Ar.; Just., A I, 53, 8; Tat.) Chaldean: γῆ Χαλδαίων (Jer 24:5) land of the Chaldeans, as the home of Abraham (Gen 11:28, 31; 15:7; in these passages: [ἡ] χώρα [τῶν] Χ.) Ac 7:4.—DEdzard, in Reallexikon der Assyriologie V 291–97. Pauly-W. III 2045–62; Kl. Pauly I 1123f; BHHW I 296f; RAC II 1006–21 (lit.).

Hdt. Hdt. = Herodotus, V b.c.—List 5

et al. et al. = et alii (and others)

TestSol TestSol = Testament of Solomon, I–III a.d.—List 2

TestNapht TestNapht = Testament of Naphthali, s. Test12Patr—List 2

ParJer ParJer = Paraleipomena Jeremiou, II? a.d.—List 2

SibOr SibOr = Oracula Sibyllina, II–III a.d.—List 5

Philo Philo = P. of Alexandria, I b.c.–I a.d.—List 5

Joseph Joseph This abbr. used when no specific texts are cited (s. Jos.); I a.d.—List 5

Ar. Ar. = Aristides, apologist, II a.d.—List 5

Just Just , II a.d.—List 5

Tat Tat , II a.d.—List 5

Pauly-W Pauly-W = AP./GW., Real-Encyclopädie der klassischen Altertumswissenschaft—List 6

Kl. Pauly Kl. Pauly = Der Kleine Pauly: Lexikon der Antike—List 6

BHHW BHHW = Biblisch-historisches Handwörterbuch—List 6

RAC RAC = Reallexikon für Antike u. Christentum—List 6

lit. lit. = literal(ly); literature (refererences to [scholarly] literature)

Arndt, William ; Danker, Frederick W. ; Bauer, Walter: A Greek-English Lexicon of the New Testament and Other Early Christian Literature. 3rd ed. Chicago : University of Chicago Press, 2000, S. 1075
